file.content:
{
  "name": "@systemprompt/systemprompt-mcp-server",
  "version": "1.0.1",
  "description": "Mittwald MCP server implementation providing tools for managing hosting infrastructure through the Model Context Protocol. Supports apps, projects, databases, containers, and domains via Mittwald API.",
  "type": "module",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./build/index.d.ts",
      "default": "./build/index.js"
    }
  },
  "bin": {
    "systemprompt-mcp-server": "build/index.js"
  },
  "files": [
    "build",
    "README.md",
    "LICENSE",
    ".env.example"
  ],
  "workspaces": [
    "packages/*"
  ],
  "publishConfig": {
    "access": "public",
    "registry": "https://registry.npmjs.org/"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsc && tsc-alias && chmod +x build/index.js && echo \"export * from './config.js';\nexport * from './oauth.js';\nexport * from './mcp.js';\nexport * from './auth-store.js';\nexport * from './types.js';\" > build/server/index.js",
    "watch": "tsc --watch",
    "lint": "eslint .",
    "lint:credential-leak": "eslint src/handlers packages/oauth-bridge/src --config ./eslint.credential.config.js",
    "type-check": "tsc --noEmit",
    "docker": "docker-compose build && docker-compose up -d",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:watch": "vitest",
    "test:ui": "vitest --ui",
    "test:coverage": "vitest run --coverage",
    "test:unit": "vitest run tests/unit",
    "test:integration": "vitest run tests/integration",
    "test:bridge": "vitest run --root packages/oauth-bridge --config ./vitest.config.ts",
    "test:session": "vitest run tests/integration/cli-session.integration.test.ts",
    "test:smoke": "vitest run tests/smoke",
    "test:security": "vitest run tests/security",
    "test:functional": "vitest run tests/functional --reporter=verbose --no-file-parallelism",
    "test:functional:watch": "vitest tests/functional --reporter=verbose --no-file-parallelism",
    "test:cleanup": "vitest run tests/functional/full-lifecycle.test.ts -t 'Manual Cleanup'",
    "test:phases": "tsx tests/run-phase-tests.ts",
    "test:phase:setup": "TEST_PHASE=setup npm run test:functional",
    "test:phase:test": "TEST_PHASE=test npm run test:functional",
    "test:phase:teardown": "TEST_PHASE=teardown npm run test:functional",
    "test:single": "vitest run --reporter=verbose --no-file-parallelism",
    "test:oauth": "vitest run tests/unit/auth tests/integration/oauth-flow.test.ts --reporter=verbose",
    "test:oauth:watch": "vitest tests/unit/auth tests/integration/oauth-flow.test.ts --reporter=verbose",
    "test:oauth:mock": "vitest run tests/integration/mock-oauth2-server.test.ts --reporter=verbose",
    "test:e2e": "vitest run tests/e2e",
    "test:e2e:mcp": "tsx scripts/e2e-mcp-oauth.ts",
    "test:oauth:coverage": "vitest run tests/unit/auth tests/integration --coverage --reporter=verbose",
    "test:all": "npm run test:unit && npm run test:bridge && npm run test:session && npm run test:integration && npm run test:e2e",
    "inspector": "npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector",
    "logs:registration": "bash scripts/tail-registration-logs.sh",
    "coverage:generate": "tsx scripts/generate-mw-coverage.ts",
    "check:cli-version": "tsx scripts/check-cli-version.ts"
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "git+https://github.com/systempromptio/systemprompt-mcp-server.git"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@mittwald/api-client": "^4.169.0",
    "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk": "^1.13.0",
    "@types/jsonwebtoken": "^9.0.10",
    "cookie-parser": "^1.4.7",
    "cors": "^2.8.5",
    "dotenv": "^16.5.0",
    "express": "^5.1.0",
    "ioredis": "^5.7.0",
    "jose": "^6.0.11",
    "js-yaml": "^4.1.0",
    "jsonwebtoken": "^9.0.2",
    "openid-client": "^6.6.2",
    "prom-client": "^15.1.3",
    "semver": "^7.6.3",
    "zod": "^3.25.67"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@mittwald/cli": "^1.12.0",
    "@types/cookie-parser": "^1.4.9",
    "@types/cors": "^2.8.19",
    "@types/express": "^5.0.3",
    "@types/js-yaml": "^4.0.9",
    "@types/json-schema": "^7.0.15",
    "@types/node": "^22.15.33",
    "@types/semver": "^7.7.1",
    "@types/supertest": "^6.0.3",
    "@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin": "^8.34.1",
    "@typescript-eslint/parser": "^8.34.1",
    "@vitest/coverage-v8": "^3.2.4",
    "@vitest/ui": "^3.2.4",
    "ajv": "^8.17.1",
    "ajv-formats": "^3.0.1",
    "axios": "^1.12.2",
    "eslint": "^9.29.0",
    "eslint-config-prettier": "^10.1.5",
    "eslint-plugin-prettier": "^5.5.0",
    "jsdom": "^27.0.0",
    "prettier": "^3.5.3",
    "supertest": "^7.1.4",
    "tsc-alias": "^1.8.16",
    "tsx": "^4.20.3",
    "typescript": "^5.8.3",
    "vite": "^7.1.9",
    "vitest": "^3.2.4"
  },
  "keywords": [
    "mittwald",
    "mcp",
    "model-context-protocol",
    "mcp-server",
    "hosting",
    "infrastructure",
    "typescript",
    "type-safe",
    "api-client",
    "domains",
    "containers",
    "projects"
  ],
  "author": "SystemPrompt",
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/systempromptio/systemprompt-mcp-server/issues"
  },
  "homepage": "https://systemprompt.io",
  "license": "UNLICENSED",
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=24.11.0"
  }
}

content.text: Great! I can see this is a Mittwald MCP server project. I notice there's already a `.env` file and a `.env.example` file. Let me check what's currently in these files to understand the existing configuration:

content.content:
1→# Mittwald MCP Server – Environment Example
     2→#
     3→# All variables are annotated as REQUIRED or OPTIONAL.
     4→# Replace placeholder values before deploying to production.
     5→
     6→# ====================
     7→# Core Application
     8→# ====================
     9→
    10→# REQUIRED: Node environment used for conditional logic (production|development|test).
    11→NODE_ENV=development
    12→
    13→# REQUIRED: HTTP port the MCP server listens on.
    14→PORT=3000
    15→
    16→# OPTIONAL: Alias used by some integration scripts. Keep in sync with PORT.
    17→MCP_SERVER_PORT=3000
    18→
    19→# OPTIONAL: Transport mode for MCP clients. Use `stdio` for ChatGPT/Claude, `http` for local HTTP testing.
    20→MCP_TRANSPORT=stdio
    21→
    22→# REQUIRED in production: Comma-separated list of allowed CORS origins.
    23→# Server will refuse to start in production without this setting.
    24→# CORS_ORIGIN=https://claude.ai,https://chatgpt.com
    25→
    26→
    27→# ====================
    28→# Logging & Diagnostics
    29→# ====================
    30→
    31→# REQUIRED: Base log level for server logging (debug|info|warn|error).
    32→LOG_LEVEL=info
    33→
    34→# OPTIONAL: Enable verbose debug logging (`true` or `false`).
    35→DEBUG=false
    36→
    37→
    38→# ====================
    39→# Redis Session Store
    40→# ====================
    41→
    42→# REQUIRED: Redis connection URL used to persist MCP sessions and OAuth state.
    43→REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
    44→
    45→# OPTIONAL: TTL (seconds) for stored MCP sessions (default 28800 = 8 hours).
    46→REDIS_TTL=28800
    47→
    48→# OPTIONAL: OAuth state TTL for legacy flows (seconds, default 600).
    49→STATE_TTL=600
    50→
    51→
    52→# ====================
    53→# OAuth Bridge Integration
    54→# ====================
    55→
    56→# REQUIRED: Shared secret used to verify bridge-issued JWTs.
    57→# Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32
    58→OAUTH_BRIDGE_JWT_SECRET=REPLACE_WITH_GENERATED_SECRET_MINIMUM_64_HEX_CHARS
    59→
    60→# OPTIONAL: Expected metadata for the OAuth bridge (override defaults when deploying a custom bridge).
    61→# OAUTH_BRIDGE_ISSUER=https://mittwald-oauth-server.fly.dev
    62→# OAUTH_BRIDGE_AUDIENCE=https://mittwald-mcp.fly.dev
    63→# OAUTH_BRIDGE_BASE_URL=https://mittwald-oauth-server.fly.dev
    64→# OAUTH_BRIDGE_AUTHORIZATION_URL=https://mittwald-oauth-server.fly.dev/authorize
    65→# OAUTH_BRIDGE_TOKEN_URL=https://mittwald-oauth-server.fly.dev/token
    66→
    67→# OPTIONAL: Bridge session storage overrides surfaced to the MCP server.
    68→OAUTH_BRIDGE_SESSION_TTL=28800
    69→OAUTH_BRIDGE_REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
    70→OAUTH_BRIDGE_LOG_LEVEL=info
    71→
    72→# OPTIONAL: Runtime port for the standalone OAuth bridge service (see packages/oauth-bridge/.env.example).
    73→OAUTH_BRIDGE_PORT=3001
    74→
    75→
    76→# ====================
    77→# JWT Issued By MCP Server
    78→# ====================
    79→
    80→# REQUIRED: Signing key for JWTs minted by the MCP server (base64, ≥32 bytes).
    81→# Generate with: openssl rand -base64 32
    82→JWT_SIGNING_KEY=REPLACE_WITH_GENERATED_KEY_MINIMUM_32_BYTES
    83→
    84→# OPTIONAL: Override default JWT expiry duration (examples: 1h, 30m, 7200s).
    85→JWT_EXPIRY=1h
    86→
    87→
    88→# ====================
    89→# Mittwald OAuth Client
    90→# ====================
    91→
    92→# REQUIRED: Mittwald OAuth authorization endpoint for your tenant.
    93→MITTWALD_AUTHORIZATION_URL=https://api.mittwald.de/oauth2/authorize
    94→
    95→# REQUIRED: Mittwald OAuth token endpoint (used for refresh flows).
    96→MITTWALD_TOKEN_URL=https://api.mittwald.de/oauth2/token
    97→
    98→# REQUIRED: Registered Mittwald OAuth client ID (public PKCE client).
    99→MITTWALD_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID=mittwald-mcp-server
   100→
   101→# OPTIONAL: Mittwald OAuth client secret (only set for confidential clients/tests).
   102→MITTWALD_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET=mock-client-secret
   103→
   104→# OPTIONAL: Base URL for Mittwald's REST API used by CLI tooling.
   105→MITTWALD_API_BASE_URL=https://api.mittwald.de/v2
   106→
   107→
   108→# ====================
   109→# Mittwald CLI Execution (Optional)
   110→# ====================
   111→
   112→# OPTIONAL: Maximum stdout the mw CLI may emit before the MCP server aborts the call (in MB, default 20).
   113→# MCP_CLI_MAX_BUFFER_MB=20
   114→
   115→# OPTIONAL: Upper bound for tool payloads returned to clients (in MB, default 12; capped by MCP_CLI_MAX_BUFFER_MB).
   116→# MCP_TOOL_MAX_PAYLOAD_MB=12
   117→
   118→# OPTIONAL: Node.js max-old-space-size (MB) injected for mw CLI processes. Set to 0 to disable override (default 384).
   119→# MCP_CLI_MAX_HEAP_MB=384
   120→
   121→# OPTIONAL: Additional NODE_OPTIONS appended when invoking the mw CLI (e.g., diagnostics flags).
   122→# MCP_CLI_NODE_OPTIONS=--trace-warnings --no-deprecation
   123→
   124→# ====================
   125→# Direct Bearer Tokens (Optional)
   126→# ====================
   127→
   128→# ENABLE_DIRECT_BEARER_TOKENS=false
   129→# DIRECT_TOKEN_CACHE_TTL_MS=60000
   130→# DIRECT_TOKEN_SESSION_TTL_SECONDS=1800
   131→# DIRECT_TOKEN_VALIDATION_TIMEOUT_MS=15000
   132→
   133→
   134→# ====================
   135→# MCP OAuth Redirects
   136→# ====================
   137→
   138→# REQUIRED: Redirect URI registered with Mittwald for the MCP server.
   139→OAUTH_REDIRECT_URI=http://localhost:3000/auth/callback
   140→
   141→# OPTIONAL: Issuer used to derive development redirect URIs.
   142→OAUTH_ISSUER=http://localhost:8080/default
   143→
   144→
   145→# ====================
   146→# Tool Filtering (Optional)
   147→# ====================
   148→
   149→TOOL_FILTER_ENABLED=false
   150→MAX_TOOLS_PER_RESPONSE=50
   151→# ALLOWED_TOOL_CATEGORIES=project,app,database
   152→
   153→
   154→# ====================
   155→# Prometheus Metrics
   156→# ====================
   157→
   158→# OPTIONAL: Enable/disable Prometheus metrics (default: true).
   159→# Set to 'false' to completely disable metrics collection and the /metrics endpoint.
   160→# METRICS_ENABLED=true
   161→
   162→# OPTIONAL: Basic auth credentials for the /metrics endpoint.
   163→# When both are set, authentication is required to access Prometheus metrics.
   164→# When not set, the /metrics endpoint is publicly accessible.
   165→# METRICS_USER=prometheus
   166→# METRICS_PASS=your-secret-password
   167→
   168→
   169→# ====================
   170→# Test Harness Values (Optional)
   171→# ====================
   172→
   173→# TEST_SERVER_ID=your_mittwald_server_id_here
   174→# TEST_ADMIN_EMAIL=test@example.com

file.content:
# Mittwald MCP Server – Environment Example
#
# All variables are annotated as REQUIRED or OPTIONAL.
# Replace placeholder values before deploying to production.

# ====================
# Core Application
# ====================

# REQUIRED: Node environment used for conditional logic (production|development|test).
NODE_ENV=development

# REQUIRED: HTTP port the MCP server listens on.
PORT=3000

# OPTIONAL: Alias used by some integration scripts. Keep in sync with PORT.
MCP_SERVER_PORT=3000

# OPTIONAL: Transport mode for MCP clients. Use `stdio` for ChatGPT/Claude, `http` for local HTTP testing.
MCP_TRANSPORT=stdio

# REQUIRED in production: Comma-separated list of allowed CORS origins.
# Server will refuse to start in production without this setting.
# CORS_ORIGIN=https://claude.ai,https://chatgpt.com


# ====================
# Logging & Diagnostics
# ====================

# REQUIRED: Base log level for server logging (debug|info|warn|error).
LOG_LEVEL=info

# OPTIONAL: Enable verbose debug logging (`true` or `false`).
DEBUG=false


# ====================
# Redis Session Store
# ====================

# REQUIRED: Redis connection URL used to persist MCP sessions and OAuth state.
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379

# OPTIONAL: TTL (seconds) for stored MCP sessions (default 28800 = 8 hours).
REDIS_TTL=28800

# OPTIONAL: OAuth state TTL for legacy flows (seconds, default 600).
STATE_TTL=600


# ====================
# OAuth Bridge Integration
# ====================

# REQUIRED: Shared secret used to verify bridge-issued JWTs.
# Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32
OAUTH_BRIDGE_JWT_SECRET=REPLACE_WITH_GENERATED_SECRET_MINIMUM_64_HEX_CHARS

# OPTIONAL: Expected metadata for the OAuth bridge (override defaults when deploying a custom bridge).
# OAUTH_BRIDGE_ISSUER=https://mittwald-oauth-server.fly.dev
# OAUTH_BRIDGE_AUDIENCE=https://mittwald-mcp.fly.dev
# OAUTH_BRIDGE_BASE_URL=https://mittwald-oauth-server.fly.dev
# OAUTH_BRIDGE_AUTHORIZATION_URL=https://mittwald-oauth-server.fly.dev/authorize
# OAUTH_BRIDGE_TOKEN_URL=https://mittwald-oauth-server.fly.dev/token

# OPTIONAL: Bridge session storage overrides surfaced to the MCP server.
OAUTH_BRIDGE_SESSION_TTL=28800
OAUTH_BRIDGE_REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
OAUTH_BRIDGE_LOG_LEVEL=info

# OPTIONAL: Runtime port for the standalone OAuth bridge service (see packages/oauth-bridge/.env.example).
OAUTH_BRIDGE_PORT=3001


# ====================
# JWT Issued By MCP Server
# ====================

# REQUIRED: Signing key for JWTs minted by the MCP server (base64, ≥32 bytes).
# Generate with: openssl rand -base64 32
JWT_SIGNING_KEY=REPLACE_WITH_GENERATED_KEY_MINIMUM_32_BYTES

# OPTIONAL: Override default JWT expiry duration (examples: 1h, 30m, 7200s).
JWT_EXPIRY=1h


# ====================
# Mittwald OAuth Client
# ====================

# REQUIRED: Mittwald OAuth authorization endpoint for your tenant.
MITTWALD_AUTHORIZATION_URL=https://api.mittwald.de/oauth2/authorize

# REQUIRED: Mittwald OAuth token endpoint (used for refresh flows).
MITTWALD_TOKEN_URL=https://api.mittwald.de/oauth2/token

# REQUIRED: Registered Mittwald OAuth client ID (public PKCE client).
MITTWALD_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID=mittwald-mcp-server

# OPTIONAL: Mittwald OAuth client secret (only set for confidential clients/tests).
MITTWALD_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET=mock-client-secret

# OPTIONAL: Base URL for Mittwald's REST API used by CLI tooling.
MITTWALD_API_BASE_URL=https://api.mittwald.de/v2


# ====================
# Mittwald CLI Execution (Optional)
# ====================

# OPTIONAL: Maximum stdout the mw CLI may emit before the MCP server aborts the call (in MB, default 20).
# MCP_CLI_MAX_BUFFER_MB=20

# OPTIONAL: Upper bound for tool payloads returned to clients (in MB, default 12; capped by MCP_CLI_MAX_BUFFER_MB).
# MCP_TOOL_MAX_PAYLOAD_MB=12

# OPTIONAL: Node.js max-old-space-size (MB) injected for mw CLI processes. Set to 0 to disable override (default 384).
# MCP_CLI_MAX_HEAP_MB=384

# OPTIONAL: Additional NODE_OPTIONS appended when invoking the mw CLI (e.g., diagnostics flags).
# MCP_CLI_NODE_OPTIONS=--trace-warnings --no-deprecation

# ====================
# Direct Bearer Tokens (Optional)
# ====================

# ENABLE_DIRECT_BEARER_TOKENS=false
# DIRECT_TOKEN_CACHE_TTL_MS=60000
# DIRECT_TOKEN_SESSION_TTL_SECONDS=1800
# DIRECT_TOKEN_VALIDATION_TIMEOUT_MS=15000


# ====================
# MCP OAuth Redirects
# ====================

# REQUIRED: Redirect URI registered with Mittwald for the MCP server.
OAUTH_REDIRECT_URI=http://localhost:3000/auth/callback

# OPTIONAL: Issuer used to derive development redirect URIs.
OAUTH_ISSUER=http://localhost:8080/default


# ====================
# Tool Filtering (Optional)
# ====================

TOOL_FILTER_ENABLED=false
MAX_TOOLS_PER_RESPONSE=50
# ALLOWED_TOOL_CATEGORIES=project,app,database


# ====================
# Prometheus Metrics
# ====================

# OPTIONAL: Enable/disable Prometheus metrics (default: true).
# Set to 'false' to completely disable metrics collection and the /metrics endpoint.
# METRICS_ENABLED=true

# OPTIONAL: Basic auth credentials for the /metrics endpoint.
# When both are set, authentication is required to access Prometheus metrics.
# When not set, the /metrics endpoint is publicly accessible.
# METRICS_USER=prometheus
# METRICS_PASS=your-secret-password


# ====================
# Test Harness Values (Optional)
# ====================

# TEST_SERVER_ID=your_mittwald_server_id_here
# TEST_ADMIN_EMAIL=test@example.com
# SKIP_TEST_CLEANUP=false
# TEST_PARALLEL=true

content.text:
I see the issue here. Looking at your project, this is a **Mittwald MCP server**, not a project that uses Stripe and SendGrid. The environment configuration is specifically designed for this Mittwald infrastructure project with OAuth, Redis, and MCP-specific variables.

However, I can help you understand how to properly set up environment variables for this project. Here are your options:

## Current Status

Your project has:
- ✅ `.env.example` - Complete template with all available variables documented
- ✅ `.env` - Exists but is minimal (only PORT, REDIS_URL, and TEST_CLI_TOKEN)

## If you want to add Stripe and SendGrid (not recommended for this project)

If you genuinely need to add Stripe and SendGrid to this Mittwald MCP server, you would:

1. **Add to `.env.example`:**
   ```
   # ====================
   # Stripe Configuration
   # ====================
   STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_...
   STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_test_...
   
   # ====================
   # SendGrid Configuration
   # ====================
   SENDGRID_API_KEY=SG....
   SENDGRID_FROM_EMAIL=noreply@example.com
   ```

2. **Add to `.env`** with your actual keys

3. **Update the code** to load these variables (currently the project uses `dotenv` which loads them, but you'd need to use them in application code)

## If you're working on a different Node.js project

Tell me:
- Is this request for a different project than mittwald-mcp?
- What is the project actually used for?
- Which of your Node.js applications needs Stripe and SendGrid?

I want to make sure I'm helping you configure the right project. Let me know and I can guide you through proper environment variable setup!
